title: adenia penangiana var. parvifolia
intro: Adenia penangiana var. parvifolia, also known as the Penang Adenia or Malayan Ahipaya, is a variety of the Adenia penangiana plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine and has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Adenia penangiana var. parvifolia include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and poultices, and its tubers are also consumed as a food source.
